title: Genki I: An Integrated Course in Elementary Japanese

lessons.title: あたらしいともだち　New Friends

examples.japanese: せんこうはにほんごです。

grammar.meaning: Add か to make a question

examples.japanese: りゅうがくせいですか。
たけしさんのでんわばんごう

contentBlocks.title: Lesson Overview
contentBlocks.text: New Friends introduces self-introductions and basic questions about names, school year, nationality, major, age, time, and phone numbers.

contentBlocks.title: Dialogue I: School Orientation
contentBlocks.text: Mary meets Takeshi at a school orientation. The exchange models greetings, stating a name, asking whether someone is an international student, nationality, and school year.

contentBlocks.title: Dialogue II: Self-Introduction
contentBlocks.text: Mary introduces herself to a group using name, school, year, major, age, and a closing greeting.

contentBlocks.title: Core Vocabulary
contentBlocks.text: Core Lesson 1 vocabulary covers school words, people, time, nationality, language, age, phone, and number expressions.

contentBlocks.title: Additional Vocabulary
contentBlocks.text: Additional vocabulary covers countries, majors, occupations, and family terms relevant to personal introductions.

contentBlocks.title: Grammar 1: X は Y です
contentBlocks.text: Explains using です to identify or describe someone or something, and using は to mark the topic when needed.

contentBlocks.title: Grammar 2: Question Sentences
contentBlocks.text: Explains making yes/no questions with か and asking information questions with words such as なん, なんじ, なんさい, and なんねんせい.

contentBlocks.title: Grammar 3: Noun1 の Noun2
contentBlocks.text: Explains using の to connect two nouns for possession, affiliation, or specification, with the second noun as the main idea.

contentBlocks.title: Expression Notes
contentBlocks.text: Expression notes cover あのう, そうですか, number pronunciation, 先生, さん, and ways to refer to the person you are talking to.

contentBlocks.title: Culture Note: Japanese Names
contentBlocks.text: Culture note explaining Japanese name order, common naming patterns, and how names are often written in kanji.

contentBlocks.title: Practice: X は Y です
contentBlocks.text: Practice describing people by nationality, year, school, major, age, and occupation using X は Y です.

contentBlocks.title: Practice: Questions
contentBlocks.text: Practice asking and answering questions about nationality, school year, age, and personal details.

contentBlocks.title: Practice: Host Family Questions
contentBlocks.text: Practice answering questions about Mary's host family using family terms, occupations, and ages.

contentBlocks.title: Practice: の Phrases
contentBlocks.text: Practice translating and describing noun phrases with の, including school, language, and relationship expressions.

contentBlocks.title: Practice: Telephone Numbers
contentBlocks.text: Pair and group work for asking, confirming, and recording telephone numbers.

contentBlocks.title: Practice: Time
contentBlocks.text: Practice telling time and asking what time it is in different places.

contentBlocks.title: Review Exercises
contentBlocks.text: Review activities combine questions about name, age, nationality, school, hometown, role play, and self-introduction.

contentBlocks.title: Useful Expressions: Time
contentBlocks.text: Reference table for hours and minutes, including common time expressions used in Lesson 1.

exercises.label: Workbook Lesson 1 Grammar 1 I: Describe Takeshi
exercises.prompt: Look at Takeshi's profile and describe him in Japanese using X は Y です.

answerKey.sourceText:
Page 34
げんきIワークブック 解答
会話・文法編 たしはにほんだいがくのがくせいです。3やま
かいわ んぼうへん
あいさつ p.13 けしさんはさくらだいがくのがくせいですか。
1.おはよう。2.ありがとう。3.こんばんは。 はい、そうです。
4.すません。5.いただきます。6.ごちそう
さま(でした)。7.いってきます。8いってら たい か
っしゃい。9.ただいま。10.おかえり（なさ I.1.ごごごじです。2.ごぜんくじです。3.ご
い)。11.はじめまして。よろしくおねがいしま ごじにじはんです。4.ごぜんよじはんで
す。12.さようなら。13.おやすみ(なさい)。 す。
14.こんにちは。 Ⅱ.[解答例〕ぜろさんぜろ(の)さんごろくよん

exercises.label: Workbook Lesson 1 Grammar 1 II: Translate Sentences
exercises.prompt: Translate sentences such as “Ms. Ogawa is Japanese,” “Mr. Takeda is a teacher,” and “I am an international student” using X は Y です.

exercises.prompt: Translate noun phrases into Japanese using A の B, including Takeshi's telephone number, my friend, English-language teacher, Yui's major, and high school teacher.
